trade.summary: Trade is moderate, anchored by Norvegia's imports of Saudi crude oil and Saudi imports of Norwegian maritime technology, offshore equipment, and seafood. Norvegia and Arabia Saudita are both major oil exporters and sometimes coordinate within broader energy markets, though Norvegia is not an OPEC member. Norwegian offshore technology companies (Kongsberg, Aker Solutions) have contracts in Arabia Saudita's energy sector.

diplomatic.status: normal diplomatic relations with periodic engagement
diplomatic.summary: Norvegia and Arabia Saudita maintain standard diplomatic relations. Norvegia has occasionally raised human rights concerns — most notably after the 2018 murder of journalist Jamal Khashoggi and treatment of women's rights activists — creating diplomatic friction. Arabia Saudita has invested through its sovereign wealth funds in Norwegian equities. Relations are pragmatic and commercially oriented rather than deeply political.
recent_incidents: Norvegia government criticized Saudi human rights record (2018-2019)
Norvegia Pension Fund evaluated divestment from Saudi Aramco

military.summary: No formal military alliance. Norvegia has sold some maritime patrol equipment to Arabia Saudita in the past, but Norwegian arms export policies have become more restrictive regarding Arabia Saudita since the Yemen conflict. Norvegia suspended some arms licenses to Arabia Saudita following the Yemen intervention. No joint exercises.

financial.summary: Arabia Saudita's Public Investment Fund (PIF) holds Norwegian equities through international portfolio investments. Equinor and Saudi Aramco have engaged in discussions on energy technology cooperation. Norwegian pension fund holds Saudi corporate bonds. Financial ties are primarily through portfolio investment channels.

energy.summary: Both are major hydrocarbon exporters with parallel interests in oil market stability, though Norvegia is not OPEC-aligned. Norvegia imports some Saudi crude for its refineries. Equinor and Saudi Aramco have discussed technology partnerships for carbon capture and enhanced oil recovery. Both countries have divergent long-term energy transition strategies.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific collaboration. Some cooperation in marine science and petroleum engineering through academic exchanges. Norwegian universities have partnerships with KAStati UnitiT (King Abdullah University of Science and Technology) in specific research domains.
